items.title: AI Mode update: Gemini 3 Flash, Nano Banana Pro
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google has been actively rolling out major Gemini AI updates throughout early 2026. The most significant recent announcement is Gemini 3.1 Pro (announced April 2, 2026), featuring upgraded core intelligence rolling out across consumer and developer products via the Gemini API, Vertex AI, the Gemini app, and NotebookLM. This followed a March 2026 wave that included Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite and Flash Live — with Flash-Lite positioned as Google's fastest, most budget-friendly model for high-throughput

items.title: Bring State-of-the-Art Agentic Skills to the Edge with Gemma 4 — Google Developers Blog
items.author: developers.googleblog.com
items.snippet: Google DeepMind and Google AI have been active on X (Twitter) in April 2026 with several major announcements. The most notable is the launch of Gemma 4, a family of open-source edge AI models released under the Apache 2.0 license on April 2, 2026. Gemma 4 supports over 140 languages, enables agentic and autonomous on-device workflows (multi-step planning, offline code generation, audio-visual processing), and is accessible via Android's AICore Developer Preview and Google AI Edge. This marks a s

items.title: Gemini API Changelog (04-01-2026) - Google AI Developers Forum
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: The Gemini API has seen significant updates heading into April 2026. The most notable recent launch is Gemma 4, Google's most capable open model family, which includes advanced reasoning, agentic workflows, multimodal vision/audio support, longer context windows, and support for 140+ languages. Gemma-4-26b-a4b-it and gemma-4-31b-it are available via AI Studio and the Gemini API. Additionally, the Gemini 3 series has expanded with Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview, and the Computer Use tool is now su

items.title: Image Generation API Reference - Vertex AI
items.author: docs.cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Imagen 4 is Google's latest image generation model, available through both the Gemini API (via Google AI Studio) and Vertex AI. The Gemini API provides access to Imagen 4 and Imagen 4 Ultra through the `imagen-4.0-generate-preview-06-06` model endpoint, with Python client examples using the `google-genai` SDK — calling `client.models.generate_images()` with parameters like `model`, `prompt`, `number_of_images`, and `aspect_ratio`. The Vertex AI path uses the same model identifier but routes thro

items.title: Gemini for Government: Build custom AI agents for unclassified work on GenAI.mil | Google Cloud Blog
items.author: cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Google Cloud has made several significant AI and Gemini announcements in early 2026. On February 19, 2026, Google unveiled Gemini 3.1 Pro — a step forward in core reasoning built on the Gemini 3 series — available in preview on Vertex AI and Gemini Enterprise. Alongside it, Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite was introduced as the fastest and most cost-efficient model in the Gemini 3 series, targeting high-volume developer workloads. The Gemini Live API, powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash Native Audio, also reached

items.title: Develop Full-Stack Apps in Google AI Studio | Google AI for Developers
items.author: ai.google.dev
items.snippet: Google AI Studio has introduced a full-stack 'vibe coding' experience that allows developers to build production-ready applications from natural language prompts alone. The platform combines a React frontend with a Node.js server-side runtime, enabling secure API calls, database connections, and npm package support—all without manual configuration. The term 'vibe coding' (coined by Andrej Karpathy in early 2025) describes the workflow: describe what you want, let the AI build it, and course-corr

items.title: Building multi-agent systems - Genkit - Firebase
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Genkit is an open-source AI framework built and maintained by Google (under the Firebase umbrella) for building production-grade AI-powered applications in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a code-centric approach to integrating LLMs into apps, with key primitives including 'flows' (observable, type-safe AI functions), tool calling, structured outputs, and multi-agent orchestration. The framework includes a local developer UI and CLI for running, testing, and evaluating flows, p
